title: System Model: Input–Output Description
summary: This page derives the input–output differential equation for the roll dynamics of an airplane, relating the roll angle or roll velocity to the aileron deflection angle. The equation accounts for the torque generated by aileron surfaces and air friction damping, yielding a second-order ODE in terms of roll angle or a first-order ODE in terms of roll velocity. A drill problem is introduced for a rotational mechanical system with a torsional spring, rotational mass, and viscous damping.

figures.caption: Attitude control of an airplane showing the aileron, elevator, and rudder control surfaces along with the roll angle φ and aileron deflection angle θ.
